title: Hydrogen-Deuterium Exchange Mass Spectrometry of the VPS13C-Calmodulin Complex
author: Matthew Parson

steps.title: Thaw the VPS13C-dATG2C-3xFLAG/CaM stock On ice
steps.instruction: Thaw the VPS13C-dATG2C-3xFLAG/CaM stock On ice . Centrifuge at 15000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes to pellet any aggregate.

steps.title: Prepare 52 µL stock solutions containing...
steps.instruction: Prepare 52 µL stock solutions containing 3 micromolar (µM) VPS13C-dATG2C-3xFLAG/CaM and either 6 millimolar (mM) EGTA or 6 millimolar (mM) Ca 2+ in Protein Buffer.

steps.title: Initiate the reaction by adding 8...
steps.instruction: Initiate the reaction by adding 8 µL of D 2 O Buffer to the protein. The final reaction volume is 12 µL with 56.7 % D 2 O.

steps.title: To prepare for proteolytic cleavage and...
steps.instruction: To prepare for proteolytic cleavage and peptide separation while minimizing back-exchange, set the climate-controlled chromatography system of an HDx-3 PAL Liquid Handling Robot to 2 °C . Keep the sample injection needle on ice.

steps.title: Wash the sample injection needle 3...
steps.instruction: Wash the sample injection needle 3 times with 100 µL of ice cold 0.1 %, Formic Acid .

steps.title: Wash the HDx-3 PAL sample loop...
steps.instruction: Wash the HDx-3 PAL sample loop 3 times by injecting 100 µL of ice cold 0.1 %, Formic Acid .

steps.title: Rapidly thaw a single HDX sample...
steps.instruction: Rapidly thaw a single HDX sample and inject the full volume into the washed HDx-3 PAL sample loop. Immediately start the UPLC and MS protocols to minimize back-exchange.

steps.title: Clean the injection needle before running the next sample
steps.instruction: Clean the injection needle before running the next sample: Wash the sample injection needle 3 times with 100 µL of ice cold 0.1% Formic Acid Wash the sample injection needle 3 times with 100 µL of 100% Acetonitrile Wash the sample injection needle once with 100 µL of ice cold 0.1% Formic Acid

steps.title: Run the exchange reaction for 00:00:03...
steps.instruction: Run the exchange reaction for 00:00:03 , 00:00:30 , or 00:05:00 at 18 °C . Exchange Reaction Timing Tables: To ensure the samples remain as similar as possible, use the following timetables along with a timer for setting the samples 3 Second Timepoint: A B 1 Sample 1 2 Off Ice 0:10 3 D2O Time 0:20 4 Quench Time 0:23 30 Second Timepoint: A B C 1 Sample # 1 2 2 Off Ice 0:10 0:30 3 D2O Time 0:20 0:40 4 Quench Time 0:50 1:10 300 Second Timepoint: A B C D E F G H I J 1 Sample # 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 2 Off Ice 0:10 0:30 0:50 1:10 1:30 1:50 2:10 2:30 2:50 3 D2O Time 0:20 0:40 1:00 1:20 1:40 2:00 2:20 2:40 3:00 4 Quench Time 5:20 5:40 6:00 6:20 6:40 7:00 7:20 7:40 8:00

steps.title: Stop the exchange reaction by adding...
steps.instruction: Stop the exchange reaction by adding 58 µL diluted ice-cold acidic Quench Buffer ( 20 µL Quench Buffer + 38 µL diH 2 O). Samples have a final concentration 0.6 M Guanidine-HCl and 0.9 % Formic Acid, pH 2.5 . HDX Sample/Quench Buffer Volume: HDX samples are always 70 μL with 20 μL of that being Quench Buffer. If the volume of Protein + D 2 O Buffer is different from this protocol, the volume of diluted Quench Buffer will be 20 μL of Quench Buffer + however much diH 2 O to bring the total volume up to 70 μL. The maximum volume of Protein + D 2 O buffer is 50 μL.

steps.title: Immediately snap-freeze the sample in liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: Immediately snap-freeze the sample in liquid nitrogen. Store samples at -80 °C until ready to run mass analysis.

steps.title: Incubate these stock solutions On ice for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate these stock solutions On ice for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Distribute 4 µL of each stock into 0
steps.instruction: Distribute 4 µL of each stock into 0.6 mL microcentrifgue tubes. Make 11 tubes with VPS13C/CaM + EGTA and 11 tubes with VPS13C/CaM + Ca 2+ . 12 pmol of protein per sample.

steps.title: Prepare the deuterated samples
steps.instruction: Prepare the deuterated samples. Create all conditions and timepoints in triplicate. This will result in 9 deuterated samples of VPS13C/CaM + EGTA (3 x 3s, 3 x 30s, and 3 x 300s) and 9 of VPS13C/CaM + Ca 2+ (3 x 3s, 3 x 30s, and 3 x 300s).

steps.title: Run the injected sample through the...
steps.instruction: Run the injected sample through the UPLC fluidics system at 200 µL, / min using 0.05% TFA.

steps.title: Digest the sample by running it...
steps.instruction: Digest the sample by running it over an Immobilized Pepsin Column (Affipro) for 00:04:00 at 2 °C . Trap and desalt the resulting peptides on a UPLC BEH C18 Pre-Column trap (Waters). Immobilized Pepsin Column (Affipro, AP-PC-001, 2.1 mm i.d. x 20 mm L x 69.3 uL V, https://affipro.cz/enzyme-columns/pepsin/ ) UPLC Peptide BEH C18 VanGuard Pre-column (Acquity, 186004629, 300Å; 1.7 µm, 2.1 mm X 5 mm, https://www.waters.com/nextgen/us/en/shop/columns/186004629-acquity-uplc-peptide-beh-c18-vanguard-pre-column-300a-17--m-21-m.html )

steps.title: Elute the peptides from the C18...
steps.instruction: Elute the peptides from the C18 Pre-Column trap over a BEH C18 UPLC column (Waters) and onto a INSTRUMENT for mass analysis. Elution is done using the following gradient of UPLC Buffer A and UPLC Buffer B: 3-10 % UPLC Buffer B over 00:01:30 10-25 % UPLC Buffer B over 00:04:30 25-35 % UPLC Buffer B over 00:05:00 35 % UPLC Buffer B for 00:01:00 35-80 % UPLC Buffer B for 00:01:00 UPLC Peptide BEH C18 Column (Acquity, 186003686, 300Å; 1.7 µm, 2.1 mm X 100 mm, https://www.waters.com/nextgen/us/en/shop/columns/186003686-acquity-uplc-peptide-beh-c18-column-300a-17--m-21-mm-x-100-mm-1-.html )

steps.title: Identify peptides from non-deuterated samples using...
steps.instruction: Identify peptides from non-deuterated samples using data-dependent acquisition following tandem MS/MS: 0.5 second precursor scan from 150-2000 m/z; twelve 0.25 second fragment scans from 150-2000 m/z

steps.title: Prepare non-deuterated samples using the remaining tubes of each condition
steps.instruction: Prepare non-deuterated samples using the remaining tubes of each condition. Repeat Step #5.1 through Step #5.4 using H 2 O Buffer instead of D 2 O Buffer.

steps.title: Prepare a high-concentration non-deuterated MSMS sequencing sample for peptide identification
steps.instruction: Prepare a high-concentration non-deuterated MSMS sequencing sample for peptide identification. Mix 10 µL of 3 micromolar (µM) VPS13C-dATG2C-3xFLAG/CaM with 60 µL diluted ice-cold acidic Quench Buffer ( 20 µL Quench Buffer + 40 µL H 2 O buffer). 30 pmol of protein per sample.

steps.title: Repeat Sample Injection (Step #8 to...
steps.instruction: Repeat Sample Injection (Step #8 to Step #12) and Protein Digestion and Data Collection (Step #13 to Step #18) for each deuterated and non-deuterated sample.

steps.title: Acquire mass spectrometry experiments over a...
steps.instruction: Acquire mass spectrometry experiments over a mass range of 150-2200 m/z using an electrospray ionization source operated at a temperature of 200 °C and a spray voltage of 4.5 kV.

steps.title: Perform mass analysis and measurement of...
steps.instruction: Perform mass analysis and measurement of deuterium incorporation using HD-Examiner (Trajan Scientific and Medical). Use the software to automatically calculate the level of deuterium incorporation of each peptide, and manually inspect all peptides.

steps.title: Analyze MS/MS datasets using FragPipe v23
steps.instruction: Analyze MS/MS datasets using FragPipe v23.1, and identify peptides using a false discovery-based approach using a database of purified proteins and known contaminants. Use MSFragger with the following settings: Precursor mass tolerance error of -20 to 20ppm Fragment mass tolerance of 20ppm Protein digestion set to nonspecific Search lengths of 4 to 50 aa Mass range of 400 to 5000 Da FragPipe 23.1 Nesvizhskii Lab MSFragger: ultrafast and comprehensive peptide identification in mass spectrometry–based proteomics. Nature Methods. Philosopher: a versatile toolkit for shotgun proteomics data analysis.. Nature methods. Escherichia coli and Sf9 Contaminant Databases to Increase Efficiency of Tandem Mass Spectrometry Peptide Identification in Structural Mass Spectrometry Experiments.. Journal of the American Society for Mass Spectrometry.

steps.title: Flush column with UPLC Buffer B...
steps.instruction: Flush column with UPLC Buffer B before running the next sample.

steps.title: Following analysis, deposit all proteomics data...
steps.instruction: Following analysis, deposit all proteomics data to the ProteomeXchange Consortium via the PRIDE partner repository. The PRIDE database resources in 2022: a hub for mass spectrometry-based proteomics evidences. Nucleic Acids Research.
